title: Dell: Applying Its Business Value Model to Storage
author: David Hill; Peter S. Kastner
subject_domain: Storage; Enterprise IT; Supply Chain Management; Business Models
methodology: Vendor interview; product analysis; customer survey; expert analysis
abstract: Aberdeen profile analyzing Dell's application of its supply chain management (SCM) business model to the storage market. Covers Dell|EMC partnership (CX product family), PowerVault NAS, tape automation, storage software bundling, and service model. Argues Dell's direct model and standards focus deliver superior price/performance and TCO/ROI outcomes for customers across enterprise, midrange, and workgroup storage.
importance_rationale: Documents Dell's storage market expansion strategy at a key inflection point; provides primary-source analysis of Dell|EMC partnership that shaped enterprise storage for a decade.
relevance_rationale: Co-authored by Kastner; detailed primary research on Dell storage strategy with survey data; exemplifies Aberdeen's vendor profile methodology.
prescience_rationale: Correctly assessed Dell's storage growth trajectory; Dell|EMC partnership continued to 2008 as predicted. Some product line predictions (AX100) proved accurate; Dell eventually became a major storage vendor.
